content:
Introduction
The modern web has evolved to shape the ways we maximize application performance. It provides us with Core Web Vitals that form the basis for how we measure our application’s performance metrics.
These core metrics act like a measuring system and also offer resources on how to enhance our application’s performance.
One of the standard methods for improving application performance is by deferring the loading of non-critical resources. Critical resources are loaded first, while less crucial resources are loaded as needed.
This strategy reduces the bundle size of our application, lowering the initial load size and improving the page’s response time.
With the new release of Angular 17, lazy loading of resources has been made possible through the introduction of the @defer template syntax.
In this guide, we will explore how to use the @defer template syntax to defer the loading of non-critical resources in our Angular applications.
Table of Contents
This post covers the following topics:

• What is @defer?

• What views can be deferred?

• @defer blocks

• @defer with @placeholder
